body: Hi — I measured my ring finger with a strip of paper and got about 2.25 inches around. What size would that be? Looking at one of your stamped silver bands.
2.25 inches around is right about a size 6. If the band's wide, go a quarter size up so it slides over the knuckle.
It's the 6mm stamped band. So a 6.25 then? Don't want it spinning.
6.25 is the right call for that width. I'll cut it to that. Ships in about two weeks.
Want to order a cuff but I'm not sure how to measure my wrist. Do I just go around the bone?
Wrap a tape measure snug around your wrist where you'd wear it, just past the bone. Tell me that number and I'll size the cuff and the gap.
Came out to 6.5 inches.
Good. I'll make the inside circumference 6.5 with about a 1 inch opening. Sits snug, slides off easy.
trying to decide between turquoise and spiny oyster for a pendant. which holds up better day to day?
Spiny oyster is harder and takes wear better. Turquoise is softer, can darken with oils over time. Both look good — depends if you want the blue or the orange-red.
leaning red. is the spiny oyster a real stone or dyed?
Real shell, natural color, no dye. I only set natural stone. I'll do the red spiny oyster for the pendant.
I bought a ring from you last year, size 7. My finger's swollen up since and it's tight. Can you take it up half a size?
Yeah, half a size is no trouble. Mail it back and I'll size it up to 7.5 and clean it while it's here.
It's got the turquoise inlay band — does that make it harder to resize?
Half a size on a band like that is fine, the inlay won't be affected. Send it when you can.
Looking at one of your buckles. I wear a 1.5 inch belt strap — will it fit, and how big is the buckle face?
The standard buckles fit a 1.5 inch strap. Face runs about 3 inches by 2.25 with the stone set in the center.
That works. Can I get it with a turquoise stone instead of the coral shown?
Yeah, I can set turquoise instead. Same price. I'll send a photo of the stone before I set it.
Question on the bolo ties — what length is the cord, and can it be shortened? I'm about 5 foot 6.
Standard cord is 40 inches. I can cut it shorter before I crimp the tips — what length do you want?
Let's do 36. And are the tips silver to match the slide?
36 it is. Tips are sterling, stamped to match the slide. I'll have it out this week.
Ordered the custom pendant on the 10th. Any update on when it ships? Hoping to have it for a birthday.
Stone's set, just polishing it. Ships tomorrow, USPS Priority. You'll have it in 2 to 3 days.
Perfect, that makes the birthday. Will I get a tracking number?
Tracking goes to your email the second I print the label. Should be tonight.
Hi Sam, Turquoise Trail here. We're low on the stamped cuffs and the small turquoise studs. Can you restock both before the weekend traffic?
Can do. How many of each? I've got six cuffs ready and can pull a dozen studs.
Six cuffs and the dozen studs is perfect. Same consignment terms as last time?
Same terms. I'll drop them by Friday morning before you open.
The turquoise on the cuff I bought has gone loose in the bezel — rattles a little. Can you tighten it?
Yeah, bezel's just opened up a hair. Send it back and I'll re-set the stone and burnish the edge. Quick fix.
Any charge for that? I bought it from you about eight months ago.
No charge. My work, I stand behind it. Just cover return shipping and I'll send it back tightened up.
Want to commission a wide cuff for my husband. How long does a custom take, and can I pick the stone?
Custom cuff runs three to four weeks. You can pick the stone — I'll send photos of what I've got in turquoise and spiny oyster.
Send the turquoise photos. His wrist is about 7.5 inches — is a 1.25 inch wide cuff too much?
1.25 inch sits fine on a 7.5 wrist, looks right on a man. I'll send the stone photos tonight.
Hi Sam, it's Michael at the gallery. We sold through the silver hoop earrings fast. Can you restock those and add a few of the drop style?
Glad they moved. I've got eight pair of hoops ready and can make four of the drops this week.
That works. Can you get them to us by Tuesday for the weekend foot traffic?
Tuesday's fine. I'll drop them off Tuesday morning, same consignment sheet as before.
Sam, the Four Corners show opens Friday. You said the saddle-stamped cuff would be ready by Tuesday — still on track? A buyer's already asking about it.
It's done. Stone's set, polished, ready. I can drop it at the gallery tomorrow before 8 if you're in.
8 works. Also — buyer wants to know if you'd take a second commission, same design, different width. I told them I'd ask.
Tell them yes. Give me their wrist size and the width they want and I'll quote it. Four weeks out.
Sam, those Sleeping Beauty cabs came in this morning. 8x10 ovals, 6 of them. You still want all six?
Still want them. Hold all six. I'll pay on pickup Thursday afternoon.
Thursday works, I'll set them aside. You want me to pull any of the Number 8 that came in last week too? Nice matrix on a couple pieces.
Send me a photo of the Number 8 first. If the matrix is clean I'll take two.
Ordering the squash blossom pendant. What chain length comes with it — 18 or 20 inches? I usually wear 18.
Comes on an 18 by default. On a pendant that size 20 lets it sit a little lower if you want it over a collar. Your call.
I'll stick with 18 then. Is the chain sterling too?
Sterling, same as the pendant. I'll ship it on the 18.
Can't decide on the stone for the ring — turquoise or coral? It's for everyday wear.
For everyday I'd lean coral — it's harder, takes the bumps better. Turquoise is softer and can wear down on a ring you never take off.
Coral it is. Is the red natural or treated?
Natural Mediterranean coral, no dye. I'll set it in a low bezel so it sits flush and won't catch.
I've got a small wrist, about 5.75 inches. Which cuff width looks right without overwhelming it?
On a 5.75 wrist I'd stay at half an inch or under. A 3/8 inch stamped cuff sits clean and doesn't ride up the arm.
The 3/8 sounds good. Can it still take a small stone?
Yeah, a 6x8 oval fits fine on a 3/8 cuff. I'll size it to 5.75 with a 7/8 inch opening.
Hi Sam — your sterling sheet order is in, the 20 gauge. You still want the bezel wire added to the same ticket?
Yeah, add two feet of the 28 gauge fine-silver bezel wire. Got commissions stacked and I'm not driving back twice.
Done. Total's $84 even with the wire. Picking up Thursday or should I hold it at the counter?
Hold it at the counter. I'll be by Thursday after I close the bench for the day.
Sam, we need a restock — the small pendants and the silver hoop earrings both sold out. Can you send a rough count of what you can supply this month?
I can do eight pendants and a dozen pair of hoops this month. I'll pull the inventory tonight and send a list Thursday.
Thursday works. Any of those pendants set with turquoise? Those move fastest for us.
Five are turquoise, three are spiny oyster. I'll mark which is which on the sheet. Thursday.
Hi — I want to order a ring but I don't know my size and there's no jeweler near me. How do I measure at home?
Wrap a strip of paper around the base of the finger, mark where it overlaps, measure that in millimeters. Send me the number and I'll convert it.
Got 54mm around. Also — do you do a wider band? This is for my husband.
54mm is a size 7. For a wide band I'd cut it 7.25 so it clears the knuckle. I make up to 8mm wide — send me which width.
My cuff got sat on and the curve's bent out maybe 2mm on one corner. Can you straighten it without hurting the stone?
2mm is nothing, I can reform it over the mandrel without touching the bezel. What gauge is the cuff, 16 or heavier?
16 gauge. Corner took the hit so the curve's off. How long would the repair take?
Day at the bench, then I ship it back. Mail it in and I'll reform the curve and check the stone's still tight. No charge on a bend like that.
